reference.definition: Details of a bibliographic or other reference to an object. The reference can be in any media eg published, unpublished, audio, video, computer record.
reference.how_to_record: Use a standard short description format agreed by the organisation.
examples: The citation order for books and journals in:
reference.use: As many times as required for an object.

reference_author_editor.definition: The Person or Organisation responsible for the intellectual content of a referenced work.
reference_author_editor.how_to_record: It will be necessary to use several units of information, including for example, for a Person, a surname and a forename. The descriptions for these information units are gathered together under the Person or Organisation headings as appropriate. The organisation may have standard forms of names for use.
examples: See under Person or Organisation as appropriate.

reference_association.definition: A single term describing the nature of the relationship between the Reference and an object.
reference_association.how_to_record: Use a single term, without punctuation or capitalisation. Maintain a list of standard terms.
reference_association.use: As many times as required for a Reference.

reference_details.definition: Details, pages, and illustrations of a bibliographic reference.
reference_details.how_to_record: Use the organisation’s agreed form of recording.

reference_note.definition: Details of the specific nature of the relevance of the Reference to the object, including an abstract if required.
reference_note.how_to_record: Use normal grammar and punctuation. The reference may be to a published, or unpublished film, sound or reproduction source.
examples: Discusses the collection in the light of disputes on attribution recently raised in Holland.
reference_note.use: Record once only for a Reference.

reference_number.definition: A number or identifier unique to a Reference.
reference_number.how_to_record: Use any reference number providing access to the work either in the organisation, local library or other source. For reference only appearing on the world wide web use the URL.
reference_number.use: Record once only for a Reference.

reference_publication_date.definition: The Date when a referenced work was published.
reference_publication_date.how_to_record: Always give all four digits for the year. Use a standard form if more detail is recorded.

reference_publication_place.definition: The Place where a referenced work was published.
reference_publication_place.how_to_record: Use the name of the city or other locality.
reference_publication_place.use: As many as required by the Reference.

reference_publisher.definition: The Organisation or Person responsible for the publication of a referenced work.
reference_publisher.how_to_record: Use the form as appears on in the referenced work.
reference_publisher.use: Record as many times as required.

reference_title.definition: The title of a referenced work.
reference_title.how_to_record: Use the form as appears in the referenced work.
examples: Spectrum: The UK Museum Documentation Standard

reference_type.definition: A term describing the nature of the Reference.
reference_type.how_to_record: Use a single term, without punctuation or capitalisation. Maintain a list of standard terms.
reference_type.use: As many times as required for a Reference.

document_location.definition: Where a referenced document is stored in an organisation’s documentation system.
document_location.how_to_record: Use a single term or code. Maintain a list of standard terms or codes. Ensure that all locations can be specifically described with unique terms.
document_location.use: Record once only for a referenced document.

catalogue_number.definition: The number assigned to an object in an exhibition catalogue.
catalogue_number.how_to_record: Exactly as given in the catalogue, using the same capitalisation and punctuation.
catalogue_number.use: Record once only for a reference if required.
